name: The Sprite Project MVP
objective: Deliver and validate the project-based LPC character-to-Godot MVP without requiring routine owner availability.

executionPolicy.unattendedDecisionRule: Choose the most reversible compliant option, record it, and continue.
executionPolicy.blockedDecisionRule: Block only the affected gate; continue all packages that can use quarantined or non-shipping fixtures.
executionPolicy.scopeRule: Implement only behavior required by the MVP journey or by a shared contract demonstrated in the MVP.

defaults.rendering: Canvas 2D with integer coordinates and nearest-neighbor sampling
defaults.godot: Pinned portable Godot 4.x runtime with generated text resources
defaults.assetPolicy: Prefer CC0 or OGA-BY with complete provenance; quarantine uncertainty

ownerInputs.request: Accept or replace the proposed CC0/OGA-BY starter-content policy after reviewing the candidate provenance report.
ownerInputs.noResponseFallback: Keep candidate assets quarantined and continue with non-shipping fixtures.
ownerInputs.cannotBeAutomatedBecause: Legal and commercial risk acceptance belongs to the owner or qualified counsel.

ownerInputs.request: Provide access to three target developers, or participate as one, for observed character-to-Godot sessions.
ownerInputs.noResponseFallback: Complete automated validation and mark the user-validation release gate blocked.
ownerInputs.cannotBeAutomatedBecause: Agents cannot fabricate independent user behavior or observations.

ownerInputs.request: Enter required credentials directly into the relevant terminal or trusted provider UI.
ownerInputs.noResponseFallback: Produce a local release candidate without publishing.
ownerInputs.cannotBeAutomatedBecause: Secrets must not pass through model context and external changes require explicit authority.

ownerInputs.request: Approve or reject public release after reviewing evidence, unresolved risks, content policy, and user findings.
ownerInputs.cannotBeAutomatedBecause: Release is an irreversible product and risk decision.

workPackages.title: Bootstrap implementation workspace
actions: Create the TypeScript/Vite workspace and module boundaries for contracts, core, adapters, app, and tests.
Pin dependencies and commit an npm lockfile.
Add format, lint, typecheck, unit, integration, and browser test commands.
Automate download and checksum verification of a project-local portable Godot runtime.
Create ignored generated-artifact and local-tool directories.
evidence: npm ci succeeds from a clean dependency directory
module-boundary checks prevent UI imports in core modules
portable Godot reports the pinned version
workPackages.gate: A clean checkout can install, verify, and run the empty product slice without global tools beyond Node, npm, Python, and Git.

workPackages.title: Inventory and qualify source content
actions: Pin candidate upstream revisions and record retrieval URLs and checksums.
Generate an inventory of dimensions, transparency, frame coverage, categories, variants, authors, sources, and license options.
Select the smallest candidate body, hair, torso, legs, feet, and optional equipment subset covering idle and walk in four directions.
Separate allowed, rejected, and quarantined records with machine-readable reasons.
Produce a candidate provenance report for OWNER-01.
evidence: checksums verify on a second retrieval
allowed records contain no unknown author, source, or chosen license
workPackages.gate: At least one technically complete starter subset exists and every allowed source has a complete provenance chain.

workPackages.title: Define minimum versioned contracts
actions: Define schemas for project, pack, asset identity, character recipe, animation, palette mapping, provenance, and export request/result.
Add stable identifiers, schema versions, pack versions, and explicit coordinate/frame semantics.
Create valid, boundary, and intentionally invalid fixtures.
Generate TypeScript types from or validate them against the schemas.
Reconcile the schemas against WP-01 real source properties before closing the gate.
evidence: all invalid fixtures fail for the intended reason
workPackages.gate: The minimum contracts describe the real starter subset without UI-owned metadata or executable pack hooks.

workPackages.title: Prove deterministic composition
actions: Load manifests and assets through stable identifiers.
Validate compatibility, animation coverage, frame geometry, and draw order.
Extract and compose idle and walk frames in four directions on integer coordinates.
Encode PNG output deterministically.
Add pixel-level and golden-image tests using real source layers.
workPackages.gate: One real recipe composes all required frames with zero unexpected pixel differences and repeatable hashes.

workPackages.title: Prove semantic recoloring
actions: Define semantic ramps for skin, hair, primary cloth, secondary cloth, leather, and metal.
Attach exact source-color mappings or explicit masks to assets rather than relying on fuzzy matching.
Implement project-theme resolution and per-character overrides.
Verify alpha, outlines, highlights, and unrelated equal-valued pixels remain correct.
Mark unsafe assets non-themeable or reject them.
evidence: recolor fixtures for every material class
workPackages.gate: Representative materials recolor across every required frame without corrupting protected pixels or transparency.

workPackages.title: Build provenance and credits engine
actions: Resolve selected assets to immutable provenance records.
Implement a fail-closed policy evaluator for unknown, conflicting, or disallowed license paths.
Generate normalized machine-readable credits and concise human-readable credits.
Deduplicate authors and sources without losing per-asset traceability.
Test mixed selections and rejected-policy cases.
workPackages.gate: Every selected layer is traceable in both credit formats and uncertainty prevents export rather than producing incomplete credits.

workPackages.title: Join the headless delivery slice
actions: Implement versioned local project persistence and deterministic save/reopen.
Expose the core through a CLI harness used by tests and future automation.
Export spritesheet PNG, animation JSON, credits, and a build manifest from one recipe.
Normalize timestamps, paths, object ordering, and encoder settings.
Run the complete slice twice in clean artifact directories and compare hashes.
workPackages.gate: One real, traceable recipe saves, reopens, recolors, composes, and exports byte-identically without UI code.

workPackages.title: Validate Godot 4 delivery
actions: Define the Godot adapter contract outside the renderer.
Generate PNG and diffable Godot 4 text resources for SpriteFrames and AnimatedSprite2D use.
Create a fixture project that imports the generated output.
Run Godot headlessly to assert animation names, frame counts, directions, speeds, and texture regions.
Use an adapter-local import script fallback if generated text resources prove unstable.
workPackages.gate: A clean Godot 4 fixture imports and plays every required animation without manual slicing or edited metadata.

workPackages.title: Build the complete product workflow
actions: Build local project create, rename, save, reopen, and recovery flows.
Build pack catalog, compatibility warnings, slot selection, and deterministic recipe editing.
Build pixel-perfect preview controls for animation, direction, speed, pause, and zoom.
Build project-theme and per-character token controls.
Build generic and Godot export flows using adapters without duplicating domain logic.
Add keyboard, focus, accessible-name, hit-testing, clipping, and visible-canvas checks.
workPackages.gate: An automated browser completes the full MVP journey against real content and verifies visible rendered output, persistence, and exports.

workPackages.title: Prove second-pack extensibility
actions: Select a structurally independent second pack or fixture with a meaningful layout or metadata difference.
Describe source differences through existing pack contracts.
Run composition, recoloring where declared, provenance, persistence, generic export, and UI flows.
Reject abstractions that require pack-specific branches in the core.
Add project migration and missing-pack recovery fixtures.
evidence: core source diff showing no pack-specific behavior
workPackages.gate: The second pack loads and exports without renderer or composition changes, and damaged project states recover predictably.

workPackages.title: Assemble release evidence
actions: Run clean-install, browser, performance, failure-recovery, and deterministic export suites.
Prepare and conduct observed target-developer sessions when OWNER-02 is available.
Resolve critical journey failures and repeat affected sessions or tests.
Bind every MVP definition-of-done claim to commands and artifacts in an evidence index.
Prepare the candidate content report for OWNER-01 and release dossier for OWNER-04.
Keep the release local until explicit publishing authority and credentials are provided.
evidence: user-session findings or an explicit blocked marker
approved content policy or an explicit blocked marker
workPackages.gate: All automated MVP claims pass, required human evidence is present, unresolved risks are accepted, and the owner explicitly approves release.

completionRule: The MVP is complete only when every work-package gate passes and OWNER-01, OWNER-02, and OWNER-04 are satisfied. OWNER-03 is required only for a distribution channel that needs credentials or signing.
